transcript: Yeah, my TV’s in need of a factory reset. I got errors, ’n defects, ’n glitches, miscoding, gon’ on with my television set.
And whenever I turn up the volume, it changes the contrast on the dang thing. When I change the channel, it changes the volume. Alright?
Okay, yeah. Who told you it needed a factory reset?
Well, how does that have anything to do witcha TV?
Well they had a sticker on the thing. It said "Energy Star" on it.
…and they said, "Get a factory reset and call this guy, here." You’re supposed to help me with this thing, here.
Yeah, it doesn’t need a factory reset. You need you somebody to come see what the problem is. But, ya know, [unclear]—
Oh, I’ll tell you what the problem is: I got quantum dots all over the place, I got a rainbow effect. When I go to turn the channel, it changes the volume. And when I go to—
So you got a bad switch.
And when I go to turn the contrast, it changes to picture-in-picture except it’s the same show, big and small.
[chuckles]This is what we do, okay?: We come out and check and see what you need. Um, what you describin’ can be a couple o’ things, but not factory reset.
That’s one thing I do know. I need an emitter, alright?
Who is this? Is this a joke or sumpin’?
No, I’m serious about gettin’ this thing fixed. Energy Star said you’d help me out.—
Okay, what is—? Yeah, I don’t know who "Energy Star" is, okay? I’ve been a technician for four—
Oh, they’re real efficient people! Real efficient! Company-wide!—
Okay, uh, whe— where are you located?
Oh, well, you— you gotta call somebody else. I’m in New Orleans. You got the wrong—
Uh, shh— I’m twenty minutes away. I’m in Bon Temps, Louisiana.
Yeah, we don’t go that far. You need to call somebody closer to you—
You just said forty minutes. I mean, twenty or forty?
Forty round trip! Are you tryin’ to trip me up or, what, are you scared of a little work?
Yeah, y— [sighs]I don’t know who this is.
This is Thorpe. And I’ve got a V-chip, now. It’s native. But with Atmos runnin’ on the thing, I think maybe that’s where I started gettin’ these nano crystals.

Okay, you know what you got? You prob’ly got a bad switch, is what you sound like. When a switch goes bad, it takes the digital and changes the [unclear] information to give the computer chip the wrong information.
Well, it takes about thirty-six hours for the TV to turn on. Listen, I’m just gonna bring this unit to you and you can check it out, alright? I’ll drop it off.
We don’t take 'em in. I’m not he— I’m no— I don’t sit in the shop.When you gonna bring it? What address?
Well I’m gonna be there in twenty minutes! Unless you won’t—
What’s the matter? You gonna call Energy Star or what?
Yeah, I’mma call Energy Star and see what they say. I’ll call you back—
I’m gonna call them on you, alright, and report ya for not helpin’ me like they said. They’re the ones who gave me your number!

Look, I don’t know what your game is. You’re not making sense, you’re saying things that TVs don’t do, y— I’m a TV man. What we do is come out and see what you need. The customers usually don’t call us and give us all this, ya know? Uh, and Energy Star, never heard of 'em.Why when I ask you questions, you won’t answer? I asked ya how old the TV was, you never answered.
You sound like you got an old TV that the switches used to go bad in.
I tried to help and you tellin’ me what’s wrong with it. If you tellin’ me what’s wrong with it, you don’t need me, you know what’s wrong with it.
If Energy Star told you to do a factory reset, all you gotta do is a factory reset. You don’t need me.
We don’t work on thirteen-inch TVs
What do you, think that’s funny or sumpin’?
We don’t work on— Wha—? [huffs] This is a joke.
Listen, my name’s Thorpe, alright? I can give ya a credit card deposit over the phone. I need an emitter, is what I need, and I need anti-blur installed on there.
Yeah, no, I can’t help you.[quietly] I don’t have an anti-blur machine, an em—
I got quantum dots everywhere! It’s distracting! It’s so distracting, I can’t take it anymore. I got quantum dots up and do—! [delay]
Listen, listen. Yeah, yeah, you need to call Hitachi, then. Call Hitachi. They’d be able to help you.
I don’t do qu— Well, I don’t do quantum dots anymore. I quit don’ quantum dots.
No! We don’t do any o’ that no more. My sub-sampler-quantum-dot machine’s broken.
Hey, man, I got after-imaging up the ying-yang over here. You got me figured out?—
Man, look, I— Yeah, I got you figured— Look, [huffs] you know, I don’t have time for this, alright? I can’t help you—
Lis— I got a native V-chip and I can’t run my Atmos on it.
